title: The Change Function
subtitle: Why Some Technologies Take Off and Others Flop
author: Pip Coburn
themes: The C/A/P/V formula for predicting technology adoption
Why consumers do not adopt products — it is not lack of marketing
The VCR adoption phenomenon and pattern replication
Anxiety reduction as the primary driver of adoption
Perceived value must exceed anxiety (V > A)
Why being cool does not guarantee market success
The role of convenience in technology adoption decisions
Predicting winners and losers in technologymarkets
When crystal balls actually work: conditions for accurate prediction
excerpt: Pip Coburn, UBS technology analyst and self-styled frog, argues that consumer adoption of new technology is driven not by marketing budgets or CEO enthusiasm but by a simple equation: perceived value must exceed adoption anxiety. When consumers have to change too many behaviors at once, adoption stalls — no matter how revolutionary the product promises to be.
